evals.prompt: I need to install the KubeSphere network extension. Please create the InstallPlan manifest for version 1.3.0 with default configuration.
evals.expected_output: A valid InstallPlan YAML manifest with metadata.name and spec.extension.name both set to network, spec.extension.version set to 1.3.0, enabled: true, upgradeStrategy: Manual, and no spec.config field because the user did not request custom values.

assertions.name: metadata_name_matches_extension_name
assertions.description: InstallPlan metadata.name must exactly match spec.extension.name
assertions.check: The manifest should have metadata.name: network and spec.extension.name: network

assertions.name: exact_version_used
assertions.description: The requested extension version must be used exactly
assertions.check: The manifest should have spec.extension.version: 1.3.0

assertions.name: manual_upgrade_strategy
assertions.description: Production InstallPlan should use Manual upgrade strategy
assertions.check: The manifest should have upgradeStrategy: Manual

assertions.name: config_omitted_for_defaults
assertions.description: spec.config should be omitted when the user wants the default configuration
assertions.check: The manifest should NOT contain spec.config

evals.prompt: Create the InstallPlan for network version 1.3.0, but enable IPPool and disable NetworkPolicy in the extension configuration.
evals.expected_output: A valid InstallPlan YAML manifest for the network extension that includes a spec.config block with global.ippool.enable true, global.ippool.type calico, and global.networkPolicy.enable false.

assertions.name: correct_extension_identity
assertions.description: The InstallPlan should target the network extension
assertions.check: The manifest should have metadata.name: network and spec.extension.name: network

assertions.name: custom_ippool_config_present
assertions.description: The config should explicitly enable IPPool with Calico
assertions.check: The manifest should contain spec.config with global.ippool.enable: true and global.ippool.type: calico

assertions.name: networkpolicy_disabled
assertions.description: The config should explicitly disable NetworkPolicy
assertions.check: The manifest should contain global.networkPolicy.enable: false

assertions.name: manual_upgrade_strategy
assertions.description: Production InstallPlan should use Manual upgrade strategy
assertions.check: The manifest should have upgradeStrategy: Manual

evals.prompt: The network extension is installed on 1.2.0 and I want to upgrade it to 1.3.0. Tell me the safe kubectl workflow.
evals.expected_output: A step-by-step kubectl workflow that first reads the current extension and InstallPlan, verifies the target extensionversion, updates spec.extension.version to 1.3.0, and explicitly warns not to keep stale pinned image tags from the old config.

assertions.name: inspect_current_installplan_first
assertions.description: The answer should read the current InstallPlan before editing
assertions.check: Should include kubectl get installplan network -o yaml

assertions.name: verify_target_extension_version
assertions.description: The answer should verify the target extensionversion exists
assertions.check: Should include kubectl get extensionversion network-1.3.0 -o yaml

assertions.name: warn_about_pinned_tags
assertions.description: The answer should mention the upgrade risk from old pinned image tags
assertions.check: Should warn to remove or update old image tags before apply

evals.prompt: I need to enable project network isolation for namespace project-a. Give me the patch workflow.
evals.expected_output: A workflow that first inspects the live namespace annotations and then patches the namespace with metadata.annotations.kubesphere.io/network-isolate=enabled unless the live cluster already proves it uses a different key.

assertions.name: inspect_namespace_before_patching
assertions.description: The answer should inspect the current namespace first
assertions.check: Should include kubectl get namespace project-a -o yaml

assertions.name: correct_annotation_payload
assertions.description: The patch payload should use the documented example key
assertions.check: Should include kubesphere.io/network-isolate: enabled

assertions.name: live_shape_caveat
assertions.description: The answer should preserve the current live object shape if it differs
assertions.check: Should mention checking live annotations before patching

evals.prompt: Create a namespace network isolation policy for namespace project-a that allows egress to external destinations. What labels must the policy carry?
evals.expected_output: An answer that identifies the namespace-level API path and preserves the exact KubeSphere labels for an external egress allowlist.

assertions.name: uses_namespace_networkpolicy_api
assertions.description: The answer should use the namespace network policy API path
assertions.check: Should include /kapis/network.kubesphere.io/v1alpha1/namespaces/project-a/namespacenetworkpolicies

assertions.name: egress_label_preserved
assertions.description: The answer should preserve the egress policy-type label exactly
assertions.check: Should include kubesphere.io/policy-type=egress

assertions.name: outside_label_preserved
assertions.description: The answer should preserve the external traffic label exactly
assertions.check: Should include kubesphere.io/policy-traffic=outside

evals.prompt: The network extension install is stuck. I need the key diagnostic commands.
evals.expected_output: A diagnostic sequence that checks extension state, InstallPlan state, target namespace, workload status, and relevant jobs or controller pods.

assertions.name: check_extension_state
assertions.description: The answer should inspect the extension object
assertions.check: Should include kubectl describe extension network

assertions.name: check_installplan_state
assertions.description: The answer should inspect the InstallPlan object
assertions.check: Should include kubectl describe installplan network

assertions.name: check_target_namespace
assertions.description: The answer should obtain the InstallPlan target namespace
assertions.check: Should include kubectl get installplan network -o jsonpath='{.status.targetNamespace}'

assertions.name: check_jobs_or_pods
assertions.description: The answer should inspect deployed pods or helm jobs
assertions.check: Should include kubectl get pods,svc -n <target-namespace> and kubectl get jobs -A
